LayerNotes: Direct official contract support now exists and reference V-SDC envelope stays supplemental
Purpose: Fiscalize invoice and return a fiscalized result
Preconditions: For E-SDC PIN and secure element availability may be required; For V-SDC client certificate and PAC are required
Postconditions: Invoice is fiscalized or a validation or operational error is returned
Notes: Reference V-SDC submitter confirms the same body contract with a connected-auth envelope built from client certificate plus PAC and optional Accept-Language and RequestId headers

LayerNotes: Official service contract and manual endpoint behavior align cleanly
Purpose: Read current SDC status
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Current SDC state and capabilities are returned
Notes: Environment URLs must not be hardcoded

LayerNotes: Official service contract now has direct evidence and manual no-card behavior support
Purpose: Read environment metadata and service endpoints
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Environment metadata and endpoints are returned
Notes: Environment URLs must not be hardcoded

LayerNotes: Transport shape and validator/status set are explicitly documented
Purpose: Verify cashier PIN against the secure element
Preconditions: Card must be present and E-SDC reachable
Postconditions: PIN is accepted and cached in RAM or a general error code is returned
Notes: Request body is plain text PIN and response body is plain text status code

LayerNotes: Direct official E-SDC availability probe contract added during reconciliation
Purpose: Probe E-SDC availability before sensitive operations
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: HTTP 200 confirms availability for the next command
Notes: Used prior to VerifyPin or CreateInvoice to reduce communication errors

LayerNotes: Resolved REC-CTR-006 split; current row is the secure-element APDU contract
Purpose: Read the last signed invoice payload directly from the secure element
Preconditions: SE applet version must support the command and CRC mode must match the applet version
Postconditions: Last signed invoice payload is returned from the secure element
Notes: Distinct from the HTTP recovery contract; the response reuses the SignInvoiceApduResponse byte layout

LayerNotes: Official APDU definition and reference sample agree on the AID and usage
Purpose: Select the Secure Element applet before subsequent SE APDU commands
Preconditions: Smart card must be inserted
Postconditions: Subsequent APDU commands are routed to SE applet
Notes: Reference implementation confirms the same AID

LayerNotes: Direct official APDU version contract exists
Purpose: Read secure element API version
Preconditions: SE applet selected or command routed to SE applet
Postconditions: Version payload is returned
Notes: Response is 12 bytes major minor patch

LayerNotes: Official APDU directive contract is primary and reference SECommand remains corroborating implementation evidence
Purpose: Forward TaxCore directive payload to secure element
Preconditions: SE version and CRC mode must match payload
Postconditions: Directive is forwarded to secure element
Notes: Reference implementation exposes SECommand primitive

LayerNotes: Direct official APDU certificate-parameter contract exists
Purpose: Read UID and certificate validity window from secure element
Preconditions: SE version must be 3.2.8 or higher
Postconditions: UID NotBefore and NotAfter are returned

LayerNotes: Official APDU contract is primary and reference samples provide payload-shape deltas by version
Purpose: Sign invoice payload and return fiscalization data
Preconditions: PIN verification and valid timestamp may be required
Postconditions: Signed fiscalization payload is returned
Notes: CRC support depends on SE version

LayerNotes: Official APDU contract plus audit-threshold rule and reference reader corroboration align
Purpose: Read current signed amount and secure element limit
Preconditions: SE applet selected or command routed to SE applet
Postconditions: Current amount and limit are returned

LayerNotes: Official version-gated AIDs should drive the canonical form while legacy reference AID remains corroborating
Purpose: Select the PKI applet on the smart card
Preconditions: Smart card must be inserted and the AID must match the smart card secure element version
Postconditions: Subsequent PKI commands are routed to PKI applet
Notes: Use A000000397425446590201 for versions below 3.1.1 and A000000397425446590009 for versions 3.1.1 and above; REF-005 currently uses the legacy AID

LayerNotes: Public verification JSON mode remains guidance-backed rather than core protocol-backed
Purpose: Read machine-readable receipt verification data from the public verification URL
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Receipt verification payload is returned without authentication
Notes: Same verification URL can serve browser mode and JSON mode

LayerNotes: Resolved REC-CTR-015 rename to official ExportCertificateApdu
Purpose: Export the taxpayer certificate from the secure element in DER format
Preconditions: SE applet selected
Postconditions: Raw certificate bytes are returned and may be parsed into UID TIN and location metadata
Notes: Official APDU contract; legacy reference naming is GetSECert

LayerNotes: Current corpus support is implementation-only
Purpose: Export the PKI certificate from the PKI applet
Preconditions: PKI applet selected
Postconditions: Compressed TLV wrapped certificate bytes are returned and can be decompressed and parsed
Notes: Reference reader inflates the payload before certificate parsing

LayerNotes: Resolved REC-CTR-017 rename to official ExportAuditDataApdu
Purpose: Export encrypted audit data from the secure element
Preconditions: SE applet selected and CRC mode must match the applet version
Postconditions: Encrypted audit data bytes are returned for local or remote audit workflows
Notes: Official contract superseding the older ExportInternalData naming used in reference code

LayerNotes: Resolved REC-CTR-018 rename to official EndAuditApdu
Purpose: Finalize the audit process in the secure element using Proof of Audit bytes
Preconditions: SE applet selected and proof-of-audit payload prepared
Postconditions: Audit session is finalized or an APDU error is returned
Notes: Official contract name is End Audit; FinishAudit remains a reference alias

LayerNotes: New core contract created while resolving REC-CTR-006 HTTP recovery split
Purpose: Recover the last invoice result by request identifier after transport failure
Preconditions: Original requestId must be known from a previous CreateInvoice attempt
Postconditions: Latest matching invoice result or null is returned
Notes: This is the HTTP recovery contract and is distinct from the secure-element APDU replay command

LayerNotes: New core contract added during audit and APDU second-pass normalization
Purpose: Read remaining secure-element PIN attempts before lockout
Preconditions: SE applet version must be 3.1.1 or higher and the SE applet must be selected
Postconditions: Remaining tries count is returned as a one-byte payload
Notes: Auxiliary SE contract discovered during reconciliation; 00 indicates blocked state

LayerNotes: New core contract added during audit and APDU second-pass normalization
Purpose: Export the TaxCore public key used to encrypt audit packages
Preconditions: SE applet selected
Postconditions: Public-key bytes are returned for audit-package encryption
Notes: Returns a 259-byte structure containing modulus and exponent

LayerNotes: New core contract added during audit and APDU second-pass normalization
Purpose: Notify the secure element that an audit session has started and obtain an audit request payload
Preconditions: SE applet selected and CRC mode must match the applet version
Postconditions: Encrypted auditRequestPayload bytes are returned for submission to TaxCore
Notes: Returns 260 bytes without CRC or 264 bytes with CRC depending on the applet mode

LayerNotes: Shared TaxCore configuration contract is explicitly published and distinct from GetEnvironmentParameters even though the response graph overlaps
Purpose: Read public TaxCore environment configuration
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Shared backend configuration is returned without authentication
Notes: The same information can also be obtained from an SDC device and the spec marks that path as preferred when available

LayerNotes: Shared Tax Rates contract is explicitly published and complements but does not replace GetStatus tax metadata
Purpose: Read public TaxCore tax-rate catalog
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Current and historical tax-rate groups are returned without authentication
Notes: The same information can also be obtained from an SDC device and the spec marks that path as preferred when available

LayerNotes: Shared Environments contract is explicitly published as a public environment-discovery surface
Purpose: List public TaxCore environments
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Available environment entries are returned without authentication
Notes: Canonical output wrapper over the top-level array of environment descriptors

LayerNotes: Shared Encryption Certificate contract is explicitly published as a public key-discovery surface
Purpose: Read the public TaxCore encryption certificate
Preconditions: None
Postconditions: Base64 encoded raw X509 certificate is returned without authentication
Notes: Transport response is a JSON string rather than an object

LayerNotes: Certificate-authenticated token bootstrap is explicitly published for E-SDC backend flows
Purpose: Request an authentication token for subsequent E-SDC backend calls
Preconditions: Valid client certificate from the PKI applet must be presented over TLS
Postconditions: Authentication token and expiration timestamp are returned
Notes: Certificate-based authentication is used only for requesting a token

LayerNotes: Initialization-command pull contract is explicitly published for E-SDC bootstrap and remediation
Purpose: Pull initialization commands for an E-SDC from TaxCore
Preconditions: Valid TaxCoreAuthenticationToken header must be supplied
Postconditions: Array of commands to execute is returned and may be empty
Notes: Canonical output wrapper over the top-level array of commands; typical initialization commands include tax rates verification URL time-server URL and TaxCore configuration

LayerNotes: Online-status heartbeat contract is explicitly published and can also return command arrays
Purpose: Report E-SDC online or offline status and optionally receive commands
Preconditions: Valid TaxCoreAuthenticationToken header must be supplied
Postconditions: Online-status acknowledgement is accepted and command array may be returned
Notes: Commands are returned only when the submitted body indicates true

LayerNotes: Command-processing acknowledgement contract is explicitly published for E-SDC command lifecycle handling
Purpose: Report whether a previously received command was processed successfully
Preconditions: Valid TaxCoreAuthenticationToken header and known commandId must be supplied
Postconditions: TaxCore acknowledges the processing result with HTTP 200
Notes: Body carries true or false depending on command-processing success

LayerNotes: Audit-package submission contract is explicitly published and paired with a documented status family
Purpose: Submit an encrypted audit package to TaxCore
Preconditions: Valid TaxCoreAuthenticationToken header must be supplied and encrypted audit package must be prepared
Postconditions: Audit verification status and optional commands are returned
Notes: Status 4 means the package should be deleted locally while status 1 is the only retryable server-storage failure

LayerNotes: Audit-proof submission contract is explicitly published and bridges Start Audit plus Amount Status to the backend
Purpose: Submit proof-of-audit request data built from Start Audit and Amount Status
Preconditions: Valid TaxCoreAuthenticationToken header must be supplied and audit request payload must be prepared
Postconditions: TaxCore accepts the proof-of-audit request with HTTP 200
Notes: The source text names this operation Submit Audit Request Payload ARP
